description: Actions and events related to the inspected page belong to the page domain.

types.description: Unique frame identifier.
Indicates whether a frame has been identified as an ad.
Indicates whether a frame has been identified as an ad and why.
Indicates whether the frame is a secure context and why it is the case.
Indicates whether the frame is cross-origin isolated and why it is the case.

All Permissions Policy features. This enum should match the one defined
in services/network/public/cpp/permissions_policy/permissions_policy_features.json5.
LINT.IfChange(PermissionsPolicyFeature)
Reason for a permissions policy feature to be disabled.

Origin Trial(https://www.chromium.org/blink/origin-trials) support.

Status for an Origin Trial.

properties.name: parsedToken

`parsedToken` is present only when the token is extractable and
parsable.

types.description: Additional information about the frame document's security origin.

properties.name: isLocalhost
properties.description:
Indicates whether the frame document's security origin is one
of the local hostnames (e.g. "localhost") or IP addresses (IPv4
127.0.0.0/8 or IPv6 ::1).

types.description: Information about the Frame on the page.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Frame unique identifier.

properties.name: parentId
properties.description: Parent frame identifier.

properties.name: loaderId
properties.description: Identifier of the loader associated with this frame.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Frame's name as specified in the tag.

properties.name: url
properties.description: Frame document's URL without fragment.

properties.name: urlFragment
properties.description: Frame document's URL fragment including the '#'.

properties.name: domainAndRegistry
properties.description:
Frame document's registered domain, taking the public suffixes list into account.
Extracted from the Frame's url.
Example URLs: http://www.google.com/file.html -> "google.com"
              http://a.b.co.uk/file.html      -> "b.co.uk"

properties.name: securityOrigin
properties.description: Frame document's security origin.

properties.name: securityOriginDetails
properties.description: Additional details about the frame document's security origin.

properties.name: mimeType
properties.description: Frame document's mimeType as determined by the browser.

properties.name: unreachableUrl
properties.description: If the frame failed to load, this contains the URL that could not be loaded. Note that unlike url above, this URL may contain a fragment.

properties.name: adFrameStatus
properties.description: Indicates whether this frame was tagged as an ad and why.

properties.name: secureContextType
properties.description: Indicates whether the main document is a secure context and explains why that is the case.

properties.name: crossOriginIsolatedContextType
properties.description: Indicates whether this is a cross origin isolated context.

properties.name: gatedAPIFeatures
properties.description: Indicated which gated APIs / features are available.

Won't repeat the enums, using string for easy comparison. Same as the

commands.name: addScriptToEvaluateOnLoad
commands.description: Deprecated, please use addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.

returns.name: identifier
returns.description: Identifier of the added script.

commands.name: addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
commands.description: Evaluates given script in every frame upon creation (before loading frame's scripts).

parameters.name: worldName
parameters.description:
If specified, creates an isolated world with the given name and evaluates given script in it.
This world name will be used as the ExecutionContextDescription::name when the corresponding
event is emitted.

parameters.name: includeCommandLineAPI
parameters.description:
Specifies whether command line API should be available to the script, defaults
to false.

parameters.name: runImmediately
parameters.description:
If true, runs the script immediately on existing execution contexts or worlds.
Default: false.

returns.name: identifier
returns.description: Identifier of the added script.

commands.name: bringToFront
commands.description: Brings page to front (activates tab).

commands.name: captureScreenshot
commands.description: Capture page screenshot.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Image compression format (defaults to png).

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Compression quality from range [0..100] (jpeg only).

parameters.name: clip
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot of a given region only.

parameters.name: fromSurface
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot from the surface, rather than the view. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: captureBeyondViewport
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot beyond the viewport. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: optimizeForSpeed
parameters.description: Optimize image encoding for speed, not for resulting size (defaults to false)

returns.name: data
returns.description: Base64-encoded image data. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: captureSnapshot
commands.description:
Returns a snapshot of the page as a string. For MHTML format, the serialization includes
iframes, shadow DOM, external resources, and element-inline styles.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format (defaults to mhtml).

returns.name: data
returns.description: Serialized page data.

commands.name: clearDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden device metrics.

commands.name: clearDeviceOrientationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Device Orientation.

commands.name: clearGeolocationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.

commands.name: createIsolatedWorld
commands.description: Creates an isolated world for the given frame.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame in which the isolated world should be created.

parameters.name: worldName
parameters.description: An optional name which is reported in the Execution Context.

parameters.name: grantUniveralAccess
parameters.description:
Whether or not universal access should be granted to the isolated world. This is a powerful
option, use with caution.

returns.name: executionContextId
returns.description: Execution context of the isolated world.

commands.name: deleteCookie
commands.description: Deletes browser cookie with given name, domain and path.

parameters.name: cookieName
parameters.description: Name of the cookie to remove.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to match cooke domain and path.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables page domain notifications.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables page domain notifications.

parameters.name: enableFileChooserOpenedEvent
parameters.description:
If true, the `Page.fileChooserOpened` event will be emitted regardless of the state set by
`Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog` command (default: false).

commands.name: getAppManifest
commands.description:
Gets the processed manifest for this current document.
  This API always waits for the manifest to be loaded.
  If manifestId is provided, and it does not match the manifest of the
    current document, this API errors out.
  If there is not a loaded page, this API errors out immediately.

returns.name: url
returns.description: Manifest location.

returns.name: data
returns.description: Manifest content.

returns.name: parsed
returns.description: Parsed manifest properties. Deprecated, use manifest instead.

commands.name: getManifestIcons
commands.description: Deprecated because it's not guaranteed that the returned icon is in fact the one used for PWA installation.

commands.name: getAppId
commands.description:
Returns the unique (PWA) app id.
Only returns values if the feature flag 'WebAppEnableManifestId' is enabled

returns.name: appId
returns.description: App id, either from manifest's id attribute or computed from start_url

returns.name: recommendedId
returns.description: Recommendation for manifest's id attribute to match current id computed from start_url

returns.name: adScriptAncestry
returns.description:
The ancestry chain of ad script identifiers leading to this frame's
creation, along with the root script's filterlist rule. The ancestry
chain is ordered from the most immediate script (in the frame creation
stack) to more distant ancestors (that created the immediately preceding
script). Only sent if frame is labelled as an ad and ids are available.

commands.name: getFrameTree
commands.description: Returns present frame tree structure.

returns.name: frameTree
returns.description: Present frame tree structure.

commands.name: getLayoutMetrics
commands.description: Returns metrics relating to the layouting of the page, such as viewport bounds/scale.

returns.name: layoutViewport
returns.description: Deprecated metrics relating to the layout viewport. Is in device pixels. Use `cssLayoutViewport` instead.

returns.name: visualViewport
returns.description: Deprecated metrics relating to the visual viewport. Is in device pixels. Use `cssVisualViewport` instead.

returns.name: contentSize
returns.description: Deprecated size of scrollable area. Is in DP. Use `cssContentSize` instead.

returns.name: cssLayoutViewport
returns.description: Metrics relating to the layout viewport in CSS pixels.

returns.name: cssVisualViewport
returns.description: Metrics relating to the visual viewport in CSS pixels.

returns.name: cssContentSize
returns.description: Size of scrollable area in CSS pixels.

commands.name: getNavigationHistory
commands.description: Returns navigation history for the current page.

returns.name: currentIndex
returns.description: Index of the current navigation history entry.

returns.name: entries
returns.description: Array of navigation history entries.

commands.name: resetNavigationHistory
commands.description: Resets navigation history for the current page.

commands.name: getResourceContent
commands.description: Returns content of the given resource.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id to get resource for.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resource to get content for.

returns.name: content
returns.description: Resource content.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was served as base64.

commands.name: getResourceTree
commands.description: Returns present frame / resource tree structure.

returns.name: frameTree
returns.description: Present frame / resource tree structure.

commands.name: handleJavaScriptDialog
commands.description: Accepts or dismisses a JavaScript initiated dialog (alert, confirm, prompt, or onbeforeunload).

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Whether to accept or dismiss the dialog.

parameters.name: promptText
parameters.description:
The text to enter into the dialog prompt before accepting. Used only if this is a prompt
dialog.

commands.name: navigate
commands.description: Navigates current page to the given URL.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to navigate the page to.

parameters.name: referrer
parameters.description: Referrer URL.

parameters.name: transitionType
parameters.description: Intended transition type.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id to navigate, if not specified navigates the top frame.

parameters.name: referrerPolicy
parameters.description: Referrer-policy used for the navigation.

returns.name: frameId
returns.description: Frame id that has navigated (or failed to navigate)

returns.name: loaderId
returns.description:
Loader identifier. This is omitted in case of same-document navigation,
as the previously committed loaderId would not change.

returns.name: errorText
returns.description: User friendly error message, present if and only if navigation has failed.

returns.name: isDownload
returns.description: Whether the navigation resulted in a download.

commands.name: navigateToHistoryEntry
commands.description: Navigates current page to the given history entry.

parameters.name: entryId
parameters.description: Unique id of the entry to navigate to.

commands.name: printToPDF
commands.description: Print page as PDF.

parameters.name: landscape
parameters.description: Paper orientation. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: displayHeaderFooter
parameters.description: Display header and footer. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: printBackground
parameters.description: Print background graphics. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale of the webpage rendering. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: paperWidth
parameters.description: Paper width in inches. Defaults to 8.5 inches.

parameters.name: paperHeight
parameters.description: Paper height in inches. Defaults to 11 inches.

parameters.name: marginTop
parameters.description: Top margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginBottom
parameters.description: Bottom margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginLeft
parameters.description: Left margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginRight
parameters.description: Right margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: pageRanges
parameters.description:
Paper ranges to print, one based, e.g., '1-5, 8, 11-13'. Pages are
printed in the document order, not in the order specified, and no
more than once.
Defaults to empty string, which implies the entire document is printed.
The page numbers are quietly capped to actual page count of the
document, and ranges beyond the end of the document are ignored.
If this results in no pages to print, an error is reported.
It is an error to specify a range with start greater than end.

parameters.name: headerTemplate
parameters.description: HTML template for the print header. Should be valid HTML markup with following classes used to inject printing values into them: - `date`: formatted print date - `title`: document title - `url`: document location - `pageNumber`: current page number - `totalPages`: total pages in the document For example, `` would generate span containing the title.

parameters.name: footerTemplate
parameters.description: HTML template for the print footer. Should use the same format as the `headerTemplate`.

parameters.name: preferCSSPageSize
parameters.description:
Whether or not to prefer page size as defined by css. Defaults to false,
in which case the content will be scaled to fit the paper size.

parameters.name: transferMode
parameters.description: return as stream

parameters.name: generateTaggedPDF
parameters.description: Whether or not to generate tagged (accessible) PDF. Defaults to embedder choice.

parameters.name: generateDocumentOutline
parameters.description: Whether or not to embed the document outline into the PDF.

returns.name: data
returns.description: Base64-encoded pdf data. Empty if |returnAsStream| is specified. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

returns.name: stream
returns.description: A handle of the stream that holds resulting PDF data.

commands.name: reload
commands.description: Reloads given page optionally ignoring the cache.

parameters.name: ignoreCache
parameters.description: If true, browser cache is ignored (as if the user pressed Shift+refresh).

parameters.name: scriptToEvaluateOnLoad
parameters.description:
If set, the script will be injected into all frames of the inspected page after reload.
Argument will be ignored if reloading dataURL origin.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description:
If set, an error will be thrown if the target page's main frame's
loader id does not match the provided id. This prevents accidentally
reloading an unintended target in case there's a racing navigation.

commands.name: removeScriptToEvaluateOnLoad
commands.description: Deprecated, please use removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.

commands.name: removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
commands.description: Removes given script from the list.

commands.name: screencastFrameAck
commands.description: Acknowledges that a screencast frame has been received by the frontend.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Frame number.

commands.name: searchInResource
commands.description: Searches for given string in resource content.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id for resource to search in.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resource to search in.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: String to search for.

parameters.name: caseSensitive
parameters.description: If true, search is case sensitive.

parameters.name: isRegex
parameters.description: If true, treats string parameter as regex.

returns.name: result
returns.description: List of search matches.

commands.name: setAdBlockingEnabled
commands.description: Enable Chrome's experimental ad filter on all sites.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to block ads.

commands.name: setBypassCSP
commands.description: Enable page Content Security Policy by-passing.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to bypass page CSP.

commands.name: getPermissionsPolicyState
commands.description: Get Permissions Policy state on given frame.

commands.name: getOriginTrials
commands.description: Get Origin Trials on given frame.

commands.name: setDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height,
window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and "device-width"/"device-height"-related CSS media
query results).

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Overriding width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Overriding height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: deviceScaleFactor
parameters.description: Overriding device scale factor value. 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: mobile
parameters.description:
Whether to emulate mobile device. This includes viewport meta tag, overlay scrollbars, text
autosizing and more.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale to apply to resulting view image.

parameters.name: screenWidth
parameters.description: Overriding screen width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: screenHeight
parameters.description: Overriding screen height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionX
parameters.description: Overriding view X position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionY
parameters.description: Overriding view Y position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: dontSetVisibleSize
parameters.description: Do not set visible view size, rely upon explicit setVisibleSize call.

parameters.name: screenOrientation
parameters.description: Screen orientation override.

parameters.name: viewport
parameters.description: The viewport dimensions and scale. If not set, the override is cleared.

commands.name: setDeviceOrientationOverride
commands.description: Overrides the Device Orientation.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Mock alpha

parameters.name: beta
parameters.description: Mock beta

parameters.name: gamma
parameters.description: Mock gamma

commands.name: setFontFamilies
commands.description: Set generic font families.

parameters.name: fontFamilies
parameters.description: Specifies font families to set. If a font family is not specified, it won't be changed.

parameters.name: forScripts
parameters.description: Specifies font families to set for individual scripts.

commands.name: setFontSizes
commands.description: Set default font sizes.

parameters.name: fontSizes
parameters.description: Specifies font sizes to set. If a font size is not specified, it won't be changed.

commands.name: setDocumentContent
commands.description: Sets given markup as the document's HTML.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id to set HTML for.

parameters.name: html
parameters.description: HTML content to set.

commands.name: setDownloadBehavior
commands.description: Set the behavior when downloading a file.

parameters.name: behavior
parameters.description:
Whether to allow all or deny all download requests, or use default Chrome behavior if
available (otherwise deny).

parameters.name: downloadPath
parameters.description: The default path to save downloaded files to. This is required if behavior is set to 'allow'

commands.name: setGeolocationOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the Geolocation Position or Error. Omitting any of the parameters emulates position
unavailable.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Mock latitude

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Mock longitude

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Mock accuracy

commands.name: setLifecycleEventsEnabled
commands.description: Controls whether page will emit lifecycle events.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: If true, starts emitting lifecycle events.

commands.name: setTouchEmulationEnabled
commands.description: Toggles mouse event-based touch event emulation.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the touch event emulation should be enabled.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: Touch/gesture events configuration. Default: current platform.

commands.name: startScreencast
commands.description: Starts sending each frame using the `screencastFrame` event.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Image compression format.

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Compression quality from range [0..100].

parameters.name: maxWidth
parameters.description: Maximum screenshot width.

parameters.name: maxHeight
parameters.description: Maximum screenshot height.

parameters.name: everyNthFrame
parameters.description: Send every n-th frame.

commands.name: stopLoading
commands.description: Force the page stop all navigations and pending resource fetches.

commands.name: crash
commands.description: Crashes renderer on the IO thread, generates minidumps.

commands.name: close
commands.description: Tries to close page, running its beforeunload hooks, if any.

commands.name: setWebLifecycleState
commands.description:
Tries to update the web lifecycle state of the page.
It will transition the page to the given state according to:
https://github.com/WICG/web-lifecycle/

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Target lifecycle state

commands.name: stopScreencast
commands.description: Stops sending each frame in the `screencastFrame`.

commands.name: produceCompilationCache
commands.description:
Requests backend to produce compilation cache for the specified scripts.
`scripts` are appended to the list of scripts for which the cache
would be produced. The list may be reset during page navigation.
When script with a matching URL is encountered, the cache is optionally
produced upon backend discretion, based on internal heuristics.
See also: `Page.compilationCacheProduced`.

commands.name: addCompilationCache
commands.description:
Seeds compilation cache for given url. Compilation cache does not survive
cross-process navigation.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Base64-encoded data (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: clearCompilationCache
commands.description: Clears seeded compilation cache.

commands.name: setSPCTransactionMode
commands.description:
Sets the Secure Payment Confirmation transaction mode.
https://w3c.github.io/secure-payment-confirmation/#sctn-automation-set-spc-transaction-mode

commands.name: setRPHRegistrationMode
commands.description:
Extensions for Custom Handlers API:
https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/system-state.html#rph-automation

commands.name: generateTestReport
commands.description: Generates a report for testing.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Message to be displayed in the report.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Specifies the endpoint group to deliver the report to.

commands.name: waitForDebugger
commands.description: Pauses page execution. Can be resumed using generic Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger.

commands.name: setInterceptFileChooserDialog
commands.description:
Intercept file chooser requests and transfer control to protocol clients.
When file chooser interception is enabled, native file chooser dialog is not shown.
Instead, a protocol event `Page.fileChooserOpened` is emitted.

parameters.name: cancel
parameters.description:
If true, cancels the dialog by emitting relevant events (if any)
in addition to not showing it if the interception is enabled
(default: false).

commands.name: setPrerenderingAllowed
commands.description:
Enable/disable prerendering manually.

This command is a short-term solution for https://crbug.com/1440085.
See https://docs.google.com/document/d/12HVmFxYj5Jc-eJr5OmWsa2bqTJsbgGLKI6ZIyx0_wpA
for more details.

TODO(https://crbug.com/1440085): Remove this once Puppeteer supports tab targets.

commands.name: getAnnotatedPageContent
commands.description:
Get the annotated page content for the main frame.
This is an experimental command that is subject to change.

parameters.name: includeActionableInformation
parameters.description: Whether to include actionable information. Defaults to true.

returns.name: content
returns.description:
The annotated page content as a base64 encoded protobuf.
The format is defined by the `AnnotatedPageContent` message in
components/optimization_guide/proto/features/common_quality_data.proto (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

events.name: fileChooserOpened
events.description: Emitted only when `page.interceptFileChooser` is enabled.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame containing input node.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Input mode.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Input node id. Only present for file choosers opened via an `<input type="file">` element.

events.name: frameAttached
events.description: Fired when frame has been attached to its parent.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has been attached.

parameters.name: parentFrameId
parameters.description: Parent frame identifier.

parameters.name: stack
parameters.description: JavaScript stack trace of when frame was attached, only set if frame initiated from script.

events.name: frameClearedScheduledNavigation
events.description: Fired when frame no longer has a scheduled navigation.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has cleared its scheduled navigation.

events.name: frameDetached
events.description: Fired when frame has been detached from its parent.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has been detached.

events.name: frameSubtreeWillBeDetached
events.description:
Fired before frame subtree is detached. Emitted before any frame of the
subtree is actually detached.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that is the root of the subtree that will be detached.

events.name: frameNavigated
events.description: Fired once navigation of the frame has completed. Frame is now associated with the new loader.

parameters.name: frame
parameters.description: Frame object.

events.name: documentOpened
events.description: Fired when opening document to write to.

parameters.name: frame
parameters.description: Frame object.

events.name: frameStartedNavigating
events.description:
Fired when a navigation starts. This event is fired for both
renderer-initiated and browser-initiated navigations. For renderer-initiated
navigations, the event is fired after `frameRequestedNavigation`.
Navigation may still be cancelled after the event is issued. Multiple events
can be fired for a single navigation, for example, when a same-document
navigation becomes a cross-document navigation (such as in the case of a
frameset).

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: ID of the frame that is being navigated.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL the navigation started with. The final URL can be different.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description:
Loader identifier. Even though it is present in case of same-document
navigation, the previously committed loaderId would not change unless
the navigation changes from a same-document to a cross-document
navigation.

events.name: frameRequestedNavigation
events.description:
Fired when a renderer-initiated navigation is requested.
Navigation may still be cancelled after the event is issued.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that is being navigated.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: The reason for the navigation.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The destination URL for the requested navigation.

parameters.name: disposition
parameters.description: The disposition for the navigation.

events.name: frameScheduledNavigation
events.description: Fired when frame schedules a potential navigation.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has scheduled a navigation.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description:
Delay (in seconds) until the navigation is scheduled to begin. The navigation is not
guaranteed to start.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: The reason for the navigation.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The destination URL for the scheduled navigation.

events.name: frameStartedLoading
events.description: Fired when frame has started loading.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has started loading.

events.name: frameStoppedLoading
events.description: Fired when frame has stopped loading.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has stopped loading.

events.name: downloadWillBegin
events.description:
Fired when page is about to start a download.
Deprecated. Use Browser.downloadWillBegin instead.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that caused download to begin.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Global unique identifier of the download.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resource being downloaded.

parameters.name: suggestedFilename
parameters.description: Suggested file name of the resource (the actual name of the file saved on disk may differ).

events.name: downloadProgress
events.description:
Fired when download makes progress. Last call has |done| == true.
Deprecated. Use Browser.downloadProgress instead.

parameters.name: guid
parameters.description: Global unique identifier of the download.

parameters.name: totalBytes
parameters.description: Total expected bytes to download.

parameters.name: receivedBytes
parameters.description: Total bytes received.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Download status.

events.name: interstitialHidden
events.description: Fired when interstitial page was hidden

events.name: interstitialShown
events.description: Fired when interstitial page was shown

events.name: javascriptDialogClosed
events.description:
Fired when a JavaScript initiated dialog (alert, confirm, prompt, or onbeforeunload) has been
closed.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id.

parameters.name: result
parameters.description: Whether dialog was confirmed.

parameters.name: userInput
parameters.description: User input in case of prompt.

events.name: javascriptDialogOpening
events.description:
Fired when a JavaScript initiated dialog (alert, confirm, prompt, or onbeforeunload) is about to
open.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Frame url.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Message that will be displayed by the dialog.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Dialog type.

parameters.name: hasBrowserHandler
parameters.description:
True iff browser is capable showing or acting on the given dialog. When browser has no
dialog handler for given target, calling alert while Page domain is engaged will stall
the page execution. Execution can be resumed via calling Page.handleJavaScriptDialog.

parameters.name: defaultPrompt
parameters.description: Default dialog prompt.

events.name: lifecycleEvent
events.description:
Fired for lifecycle events (navigation, load, paint, etc) in the current
target (including local frames).

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: Loader identifier. Empty string if the request is fetched from worker.

events.name: backForwardCacheNotUsed
events.description:
Fired for failed bfcache history navigations if BackForwardCache feature is enabled. Do
not assume any ordering with the Page.frameNavigated event. This event is fired only for
main-frame history navigation where the document changes (non-same-document navigations),
when bfcache navigation fails.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: The loader id for the associated navigation.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: The frame id of the associated frame.

parameters.name: notRestoredExplanations
parameters.description: Array of reasons why the page could not be cached. This must not be empty.

parameters.name: notRestoredExplanationsTree
parameters.description: Tree structure of reasons why the page could not be cached for each frame.

events.name: navigatedWithinDocument
events.description: Fired when same-document navigation happens, e.g. due to history API usage or anchor navigation.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Frame's new url.

parameters.name: navigationType
parameters.description: Navigation type

events.name: screencastFrame
events.description: Compressed image data requested by the `startScreencast`.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Base64-encoded compressed image. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: Screencast frame metadata.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Frame number.

events.name: screencastVisibilityChanged
events.description: Fired when the page with currently enabled screencast was shown or hidden `.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: True if the page is visible.

events.name: windowOpen
events.description:
Fired when a new window is going to be opened, via window.open(), link click, form submission,
etc.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL for the new window.

parameters.name: windowName
parameters.description: Window name.

parameters.name: windowFeatures
parameters.description: An array of enabled window features.

parameters.name: userGesture
parameters.description: Whether or not it was triggered by user gesture.

events.name: compilationCacheProduced
events.description: Issued for every compilation cache generated.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Base64-encoded data (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)
